concerns.text: FR-006 and Spec Assumptions require validation against 'out-of-sample held-out recession periods' (plural), explicitly citing the 2008 Global Financial Crisis and 2020 COVID-19. Task T036 hardcodes the validation window to '2020-2022' only. There is no task to validate against the 2008 period or to implement configurable recession period selection, leaving a critical functional requirement unmet.

SC-006 requires sensitivity analysis to mask a specific proportion of data ('[deferred] to [deferred]') and report the shift. Tasks T035 and T050 implement sensitivity analysis but lack a specific task to define, configure, or log the exact proportion of data masked. Without a task to implement the configuration/logging of this proportion, the success criterion (measuring the shift against a defined proportion) is unmeasurable.

Plan.md Phase 1 and Spec Edge Cases mandate a 'Johansen Cointegration Test' to decide between VAR and VECM paths. While T025 implements the test, there is no explicit task to generate the `data/processed/model_spec.json` decision artifact that logs the cointegration result and the resulting model path (VAR vs VECM) before modeling begins. T029 generates logs but does not explicitly link the decision logic to the VECM path generation.

FR-006 requires Moving Block Bootstrap (MBB) validation. T034 implements MBB, but T051 duplicates the verification logic for the CI width consistency metric (≤20% of OLS coefficient). This redundancy suggests a potential gap in the single source of truth for the metric calculation or a missing task to consolidate the verification logic, risking inconsistent implementation of the SC-004 requirement.

Plan.md 'Data Resource Strategy' states that CI runs may use synthetic data, and artifacts will include a 'data_source' flag. SC-004 requires MBB consistency checks. There is no task to handle the 'Synthetic' data flag to adjust or skip the robustness threshold check (≤20% CI width) when synthetic data is used, potentially causing false failures or invalid metric reporting in CI environments.
